1Ôćĺ/**
     2Ôćĺ * Typed query functions ÔÇö centralized DB reads for all route handlers.
     3Ôćĺ *
     4Ôćĺ * Every `as` cast lives here (once per query), not in routes.
     5Ôćĺ * Route handlers call these functions instead of inline SQL.
     6Ôćĺ */
     7Ôćĺimport type Database from "better-sqlite3";
     8Ôćĺimport type {
     9Ôćĺ  RepositoryRow,
    10Ôćĺ  RepositoryWithCountsRow,
    11Ôćĺ  WorkspaceRow,
    12Ôćĺ  WorkspaceWithDetailsRow,
    13Ôćĺ  SessionRow,
    14Ôćĺ  SessionWithDetailsRow,
    15Ôćĺ  MessageRow,
    16Ôćĺ  StatsRow,
    17Ôćĺ} from "./types";
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ// ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ Workspace Queries ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ/**
    22Ôćĺ * Canonical workspace + repo + session JOIN.
    23Ôćĺ * Single source of truth ÔÇö reused by list, get-by-id, and by-repo queries.
    24Ôćĺ */
    25Ôćĺconst WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT = `
    26Ôćĺ  SELECT
    27Ôćĺ    w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
    28Ôćĺ    w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.status, w.current_session_id,
    29Ôćĺ    w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
    30Ôćĺ    w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
    31Ôćĺ    w.updated_at,
    32Ôćĺ    r.name as repo_name, r.root_path, r.git_default_branch,
    33Ôćĺ    s.status as session_status, s.model,
    34Ôćĺ    s.error_category as session_error_category,
    35Ôćĺ    s.error_message as session_error_message,
    36Ôćĺ    s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
    37Ôćĺ  FROM workspaces w
    38Ôćĺ  LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
    39Ôćĺ  LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
    40Ôćĺ`;
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺexport function getAllWorkspaces(db: Database.Database): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
    43Ôćĺ  return db
    44Ôćĺ    .prepare(
    45Ôćĺ      `
    46Ôćĺ    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
    47Ôćĺ    ORDER BY w.updated_at DESC
    48Ôćĺ    LIMIT 100
    49Ôćĺ  `
    50Ôćĺ    )
    51Ôćĺ    .all() as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
    52Ôćĺ}
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺexport function getWorkspacesByRepo(
    55Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
    56Ôćĺ  state?: string
    57Ôćĺ): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
    58Ôćĺ  // Support comma-separated states (e.g. "ready,initializing")
    59Ôćĺ  let stateFilter = "";
    60Ôćĺ  let stateParams: string[] = [];
    61Ôćĺ  if (state) {
    62Ôćĺ    const states = state.split(",").map((s) => s.trim());
    63Ôćĺ    stateFilter = `WHERE w.state IN (${states.map(() => "?").join(",")})`;
    64Ôćĺ    stateParams = states;
    65Ôćĺ  }
    66Ôćĺ  return db
    67Ôćĺ    .prepare(
    68Ôćĺ      `
    69Ôćĺ    SELECT
    70Ôćĺ      w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
    71Ôćĺ      w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.status, w.current_session_id,
    72Ôćĺ      w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
    73Ôćĺ      w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
    74Ôćĺ      w.updated_at,
    75Ôćĺ      r.name as repo_name, r.sort_order as repo_sort_order, r.root_path,
    76Ôćĺ      r.git_default_branch,
    77Ôćĺ      s.status as session_status, s.model,
    78Ôćĺ      s.error_category as session_error_category,
    79Ôćĺ      s.error_message as session_error_message,
    80Ôćĺ      s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
    81Ôćĺ    FROM workspaces w
    82Ôćĺ    LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
    83Ôćĺ    LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
    84Ôćĺ    ${stateFilter}
    85Ôćĺ    ORDER BY r.sort_order, r.name, w.updated_at DESC
    86Ôćĺ  `
    87Ôćĺ    )
    88Ôćĺ    .all(...stateParams) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
    89Ôćĺ}
    90Ôćĺ
    91Ôćĺexport function getWorkspaceById(
    92Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
    93Ôćĺ  id: string
    94Ôćĺ): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined {
    95Ôćĺ  return db
    96Ôćĺ    .prepare(
    97Ôćĺ      `
    98Ôćĺ    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
    99Ôćĺ    WHERE w.id = ?
   100Ôćĺ  `
   101Ôćĺ    )
   102Ôćĺ    .get(id) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined;
   103Ôćĺ}
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ/** Used by withWorkspace middleware ÔÇö lighter query without session JOIN. */
   106Ôćĺexport function getWorkspaceForMiddleware(
   107Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
   108Ôćĺ  id: string
   109Ôćĺ): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined {
   110Ôćĺ  return db
   111Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   112Ôćĺ      `
   113Ôćĺ    SELECT w.*, r.root_path, r.git_default_branch, r.name as repo_name
   114Ôćĺ    FROM workspaces w
   115Ôćĺ    LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
   116Ôćĺ    WHERE w.id = ?
   117Ôćĺ  `
   118Ôćĺ    )
   119Ôćĺ    .get(id) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined;
   120Ôćĺ}
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺexport function getWorkspaceRaw(db: Database.Database, id: string): WorkspaceRow | undefined {
   123Ôćĺ  return db.prepare("SELECT * FROM workspaces WHERE id = ?").get(id) as WorkspaceRow | undefined;
   124Ôćĺ}
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ/**
   127Ôćĺ * Look up workspaces by their current session IDs.
   128Ôćĺ * Used by the query engine for delta pushes ÔÇö when a session status changes,
   129Ôćĺ * we only need the affected workspace(s) instead of re-querying everything.
   130Ôćĺ */
   131Ôćĺexport function getWorkspacesBySessionIds(
   132Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
   133Ôćĺ  sessionIds: string[]
   134Ôćĺ): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
   135Ôćĺ  if (sessionIds.length === 0) return [];
   136Ôćĺ  const placeholders = sessionIds.map(() => "?").join(",");
   137Ôćĺ  return db
   138Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   139Ôćĺ      `
   140Ôćĺ    SELECT
   141Ôćĺ      w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
   142Ôćĺ      w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.status, w.current_session_id,
   143Ôćĺ      w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
   144Ôćĺ      w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
   145Ôćĺ      w.updated_at,
   146Ôćĺ      r.name as repo_name, r.sort_order as repo_sort_order, r.root_path,
   147Ôćĺ      r.git_default_branch,
   148Ôćĺ      s.status as session_status, s.model,
   149Ôćĺ      s.error_category as session_error_category,
   150Ôćĺ      s.error_message as session_error_message,
   151Ôćĺ      s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
   152Ôćĺ    FROM workspaces w
   153Ôćĺ    LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
   154Ôćĺ    LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
   155Ôćĺ    WHERE w.current_session_id IN (${placeholders})
   156Ôćĺ  `
   157Ôćĺ    )
   158Ôćĺ    .all(...sessionIds) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
   159Ôćĺ}
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ/**
   162Ôćĺ * Non-archived workspaces for query engine and relay clients.
   163Ôćĺ * Used by: query-engine snapshots, relay initial state, relay data requests.
   164Ôćĺ */
   165Ôćĺexport function getDashboardWorkspaces(db: Database.Database): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
   166Ôćĺ  return db
   167Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   168Ôćĺ      `
   169Ôćĺ    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
   170Ôćĺ    WHERE w.state != 'archived'
   171Ôćĺ    ORDER BY r.sort_order ASC, r.name ASC, w.updated_at DESC
   172Ôćĺ  `
   173Ôćĺ    )
   174Ôćĺ    .all() as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
   175Ôćĺ}
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ// ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ Session Queries ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ
   178Ôćĺ
   179Ôćĺ/** Coerce SQLite INTEGER booleans (0/1) to JS booleans so HTTP and IPC return the same shape. */
   180Ôćĺfunction coerceSessionBooleans<T extends SessionRow>(row: T): T {
   181Ôćĺ  return { ...row, is_hidden: Boolean(row.is_hidden) };
   182Ôćĺ}
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺ/**
   185Ôćĺ * Session query ÔÇö uses denormalized message_count column
   186Ôćĺ * instead of COUNT(m.id) JOIN, eliminating expensive aggregation.
   187Ôćĺ */
   188Ôćĺconst SESSION_DETAILS_SELECT = `
   189Ôćĺ  SELECT s.*, w.slug, w.state as workspace_state
   190Ôćĺ  FROM sessions s
   191Ôćĺ  LEFT JOIN workspaces w ON s.id = w.current_session_id
   192Ôćĺ`;
   193Ôćĺ
   194Ôćĺexport function getAllSessions(db: Database.Database): SessionWithDetailsRow[] {
   195Ôćĺ  const rows = db
   196Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   197Ôćĺ      `
   198Ôćĺ    ${SESSION_DETAILS_SELECT}
   199Ôćĺ    ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC
   200Ôćĺ    LIMIT 50
   201Ôćĺ  `
   202Ôćĺ    )
   203Ôćĺ    .all() as SessionWithDetailsRow[];
   204Ôćĺ  return rows.map(coerceSessionBooleans);
   205Ôćĺ}
   206Ôćĺ
   207Ôćĺexport function getSessionById(
   208Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
   209Ôćĺ  id: string
   210Ôćĺ): SessionWithDetailsRow | undefined {
   211Ôćĺ  const row = db
   212Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   213Ôćĺ      `
   214Ôćĺ    ${SESSION_DETAILS_SELECT}
   215Ôćĺ    WHERE s.id = ?
   216Ôćĺ  `
   217Ôćĺ    )
   218Ôćĺ    .get(id) as SessionWithDetailsRow | undefined;
   219Ôćĺ  return row ? coerceSessionBooleans(row) : undefined;
   220Ôćĺ}
   221Ôćĺ
   222Ôćĺexport function getSessionRaw(db: Database.Database, id: string): SessionRow | undefined {
   223Ôćĺ  const row = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?").get(id) as SessionRow | undefined;
   224Ôćĺ  return row ? coerceSessionBooleans(row) : undefined;
   225Ôćĺ}
   226Ôćĺ
   227Ôćĺ/** All sessions for a workspace, ordered by creation time (UUID7 is chronological). */
   228Ôćĺexport function getSessionsByWorkspaceId(db: Database.Database, workspaceId: string): SessionRow[] {
   229Ôćĺ  const rows = db
   230Ôćĺ    .prepare("SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE workspace_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC")
   231Ôćĺ    .all(workspaceId) as SessionRow[];
   232Ôćĺ  return rows.map(coerceSessionBooleans);
   233Ôćĺ}
   234Ôćĺ
   235Ôćĺ// ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ Message Queries ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ
   236Ôćĺ
   237Ôćĺ/**
   238Ôćĺ * Fetch paginated messages for a session.
   239Ôćĺ * Uses seq (per-session monotonic integer) as cursor ÔÇö no collisions,
   240Ôćĺ * no NULLs, integer comparison. sent_at had 15K+ collisions in production.
   241Ôćĺ */
   242Ôćĺexport function getMessages(
   243Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
   244Ôćĺ  sessionId: string,
   245Ôćĺ  opts: { limit: number; before?: number; after?: number }
   246Ôćĺ): MessageRow[] {
   247Ôćĺ  if (opts.before) {
   248Ôćĺ    // Load older: fetch N messages before seq, then re-sort ascending
   249Ôćĺ    return db
   250Ôćĺ      .prepare(
   251Ôćĺ        `
   252Ôćĺ      SELECT * FROM (
   253Ôćĺ        SELECT * FROM messages
   254Ôćĺ        WHERE session_id = ? AND seq < ?
   255Ôćĺ        ORDER BY seq DESC
   256Ôćĺ        LIMIT ?
   257Ôćĺ      ) sub ORDER BY seq ASC
   258Ôćĺ    `
   259Ôćĺ      )
   260Ôćĺ      .all(sessionId, opts.before, opts.limit) as MessageRow[];
   261Ôćĺ  }
   262Ôćĺ
   263Ôćĺ  if (opts.after) {
   264Ôćĺ    // Load newer: fetch N messages after seq, already in ascending order
   265Ôćĺ    return db
   266Ôćĺ      .prepare(
   267Ôćĺ        `
   268Ôćĺ      SELECT * FROM messages
   269Ôćĺ      WHERE session_id = ? AND seq > ?
   270Ôćĺ      ORDER BY seq ASC
   271Ôćĺ      LIMIT ?
   272Ôćĺ    `
   273Ôćĺ      )
   274Ôćĺ      .all(sessionId, opts.after, opts.limit) as MessageRow[];
   275Ôćĺ  }
   276Ôćĺ
   277Ôćĺ  // Default: load latest N messages (DESC then re-wrap ASC)
   278Ôćĺ  return db
   279Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   280Ôćĺ      `
   281Ôćĺ    SELECT * FROM (
   282Ôćĺ      SELECT * FROM messages
   283Ôćĺ      WHERE session_id = ?
   284Ôćĺ      ORDER BY seq DESC
   285Ôćĺ      LIMIT ?
   286Ôćĺ    ) sub ORDER BY seq ASC
   287Ôćĺ  `
   288Ôćĺ    )
   289Ôćĺ    .all(sessionId, opts.limit) as MessageRow[];
   290Ôćĺ}
   291Ôćĺ
   292Ôćĺexport function hasOlderMessages(db: Database.Database, sessionId: string, seq: number): boolean {
   293Ôćĺ  return !!db
   294Ôćĺ    .prepare("SELECT 1 FROM messages WHERE session_id = ? AND seq < ? LIMIT 1")
   295Ôćĺ    .get(sessionId, seq);
   296Ôćĺ}
   297Ôćĺ
   298Ôćĺexport function hasNewerMessages(db: Database.Database, sessionId: string, seq: number): boolean {
   299Ôćĺ  return !!db
   300Ôćĺ    .prepare("SELECT 1 FROM messages WHERE session_id = ? AND seq > ? LIMIT 1")
   301Ôćĺ    .get(sessionId, seq);
   302Ôćĺ}
   303Ôćĺ
   304Ôćĺexport function getLastAssistantAgentMessageId(
   305Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
   306Ôćĺ  sessionId: string
   307Ôćĺ): string | null {
   308Ôćĺ  const row = db
   309Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   310Ôćĺ      `
   311Ôćĺ    SELECT agent_message_id FROM messages
   312Ôćĺ    WHERE session_id = ? AND role = 'assistant' AND agent_message_id IS NOT NULL
   313Ôćĺ    ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1
   314Ôćĺ  `
   315Ôćĺ    )
   316Ôćĺ    .get(sessionId) as { agent_message_id: string } | undefined;
   317Ôćĺ  return row?.agent_message_id ?? null;
   318Ôćĺ}
   319Ôćĺ
   320Ôćĺexport function getMessageById(db: Database.Database, id: string): MessageRow | undefined {
   321Ôćĺ  return db.prepare("SELECT * FROM messages WHERE id = ?").get(id) as MessageRow | undefined;
   322Ôćĺ}
   323Ôćĺ
   324Ôćĺ/** Get the highest seq for a session (cursor initialization for real-time streaming). */
   325Ôćĺexport function getMaxMessageSeq(db: Database.Database, sessionId: string): number {
   326Ôćĺ  const row = db
   327Ôćĺ    .prepare("SELECT COALESCE(MAX(seq), 0) as max_seq FROM messages WHERE session_id = ?")
   328Ôćĺ    .get(sessionId) as { max_seq: number } | undefined;
   329Ôćĺ  return row?.max_seq ?? 0;
   330Ôćĺ}
   331Ôćĺ
   332Ôćĺ/** Fetch all messages after a given seq (delta push for real-time streaming). */
   333Ôćĺexport function getMessagesDelta(
   334Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
   335Ôćĺ  sessionId: string,
   336Ôćĺ  afterSeq: number
   337Ôćĺ): MessageRow[] {
   338Ôćĺ  return db
   339Ôćĺ    .prepare("SELECT * FROM messages WHERE session_id = ? AND seq > ? ORDER BY seq ASC")
   340Ôćĺ    .all(sessionId, afterSeq) as MessageRow[];
   341Ôćĺ}
   342Ôćĺ
   343Ôćĺ// ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ Repository Queries ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ
   344Ôćĺ
   345Ôćĺexport function getAllRepositories(db: Database.Database): RepositoryWithCountsRow[] {
   346Ôćĺ  return db
   347Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   348Ôćĺ      `
   349Ôćĺ    SELECT r.*,
   350Ôćĺ           COUNT(CASE WHEN w.state = 'ready' THEN 1 END) as ready_count,
   351Ôćĺ           COUNT(CASE WHEN w.state = 'archived' THEN 1 END) as archived_count,
   352Ôćĺ           COUNT(w.id) as total_count
   353Ôćĺ    FROM repositories r
   354Ôćĺ    LEFT JOIN workspaces w ON w.repository_id = r.id
   355Ôćĺ    GROUP BY r.id
   356Ôćĺ    ORDER BY r.sort_order, r.name
   357Ôćĺ  `
   358Ôćĺ    )
   359Ôćĺ    .all() as RepositoryWithCountsRow[];
   360Ôćĺ}
   361Ôćĺ
   362Ôćĺ/** Lightweight repo list for backfilling empty groups in by-repo queries. */
   363Ôćĺexport function getAllRepositorySummaries(
   364Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database
   365Ôćĺ): { id: string; name: string; sort_order: number }[] {
   366Ôćĺ  return db
   367Ôćĺ    .prepare("SELECT id, name, sort_order FROM repositories ORDER BY sort_order, name")
   368Ôćĺ    .all() as { id: string; name: string; sort_order: number }[];
   369Ôćĺ}
   370Ôćĺ
   371Ôćĺexport function getRepositoryById(db: Database.Database, id: string): RepositoryRow | undefined {
   372Ôćĺ  return db.prepare("SELECT * FROM repositories WHERE id = ?").get(id) as RepositoryRow | undefined;
   373Ôćĺ}
   374Ôćĺ
   375Ôćĺexport function getRepositoryByRootPath(
   376Ôćĺ  db: Database.Database,
   377Ôćĺ  rootPath: string
   378Ôćĺ): RepositoryRow | undefined {
   379Ôćĺ  return db.prepare("SELECT * FROM repositories WHERE root_path = ?").get(rootPath) as
   380Ôćĺ    | RepositoryRow
   381Ôćĺ    | undefined;
   382Ôćĺ}
   383Ôćĺ
   384Ôćĺexport function getMaxRepositorySortOrder(db: Database.Database): number {
   385Ôćĺ  const row = db.prepare("SELECT MAX(sort_order) as max FROM repositories").get() as {
   386Ôćĺ    max: number | null;
   387Ôćĺ  };
   388Ôćĺ  return row?.max ?? 0;
   389Ôćĺ}
   390Ôćĺ
   391Ôćĺ// ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ Stats Queries ÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇ
   392Ôćĺ
   393Ôćĺlet cachedStats: StatsRow | null = null;
   394Ôćĺlet cachedAt = 0;
   395Ôćĺconst STATS_TTL_MS = 5_000;
   396Ôćĺ
   397Ôćĺexport function getStats(db: Database.Database): StatsRow {
   398Ôćĺ  const now = Date.now();
   399Ôćĺ  if (cachedStats && now - cachedAt < STATS_TTL_MS) return cachedStats;
   400Ôćĺ  cachedStats = db
   401Ôćĺ    .prepare(
   402Ôćĺ      `
   403Ôćĺ    SELECT
   404Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces) as workspaces,
   405Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE state = 'ready') as workspaces_ready,
   406Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE state = 'archived') as workspaces_archived,
   407Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE status = 'backlog' AND state != 'archived') as workspaces_backlog,
   408Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE status = 'in-progress' AND state != 'archived') as workspaces_in_progress,
   409Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE status = 'in-review' AND state != 'archived') as workspaces_in_review,
   410Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM repositories) as repositories,
   411Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions) as sessions,
   412Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE status = 'idle') as sessions_idle,
   413Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE status = 'working') as sessions_working,
   414Ôćĺ      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages) as messages
   415Ôćĺ  `
   416Ôćĺ    )
   417Ôćĺ    .get() as StatsRow;
   418Ôćĺ  cachedAt = now;
   419Ôćĺ  return cachedStats;
   420Ôćĺ}
   421Ôćĺ
   422Ôćĺ/** Reset the in-memory stats cache (for testing). */
   423Ôćĺexport function resetStatsCache(): void {
   424Ôćĺ  cachedStats = null;
   425Ôćĺ  cachedAt = 0;
   426Ôćĺ}
   427Ôćĺ
   428Ôćĺ/**
   429Ôćĺ * Backfill workspace status for existing databases after migration.
   430Ôćĺ * Idempotent ÔÇö only updates rows still at the 'in-progress' default.
   431Ôćĺ */
   432Ôćĺexport function backfillWorkspaceStatus(db: Database.Database): void {
   433Ôćĺ  // Archived with commits Ôćĺ done (work was completed)
   434Ôćĺ  db.prepare(`
   435Ôćĺ    UPDATE workspaces SET status = 'done'
   436Ôćĺ    WHERE state = 'archived' AND archive_commit IS NOT NULL AND status = 'in-progress'
   437Ôćĺ  `).run();
   438Ôćĺ  // Archived without commits Ôćĺ canceled (abandoned)
   439Ôćĺ  db.prepare(`
   440Ôćĺ    UPDATE workspaces SET status = 'canceled'
   441Ôćĺ    WHERE state = 'archived' AND archive_commit IS NULL AND status = 'in-progress'
   442Ôćĺ  `).run();
   443Ôćĺ  // Has PR URL Ôćĺ in-review (if still active)
   444Ôćĺ  db.prepare(`
   445Ôćĺ    UPDATE workspaces SET status = 'in-review'
   446Ôćĺ    WHERE pr_url IS NOT NULL AND state = 'ready' AND status = 'in-progress'
   447Ôćĺ  `).run();
   448Ôćĺ}
   449Ôćĺ

content.text:
Fix hardcoded Tailwind palette colors in the workspace status config. Working directory: /Users/zvada/Developer/agent/box-ide/.conductor/da-nang-v3

## Problem

`WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` in `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` uses `text-amber-500`, `text-purple-500`, `text-green-500` ÔÇö raw Tailwind palette utilities. The project's CLAUDE.md says "Never hardcode colors at all costs" and the adjacent `STATUS_CONFIG` in the same file uses semantic tokens (`text-destructive`, `text-status-unread`, `text-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`).

## Fix

1. Read `apps/web/src/global.css` to understand the existing CSS variable structure (look for `:root`, `.dark`, `@theme` blocks, and existing `--status-*` variables)
2. Add three new CSS variables for workflow status colors. Place them near existing status color variables. Name them `--status-in-progress`, `--status-in-review`, `--status-done`. For values:
   - `--status-in-progress` should be amber/warning-like (check if there's an existing `--warning` variable to reference, otherwise use an oklch amber)
   - `--status-in-review` should be purple (check if there's a `--primary` or purple variable, otherwise use oklch purple)
   - `--status-done` should be green/success-like (check if there's a `--success` variable, otherwise use oklch green)
   - Define in both `:root` (light) and `.dark` (dark) themes
3. Add the corresponding `--color-status-*` entries in the `@theme` block so Tailwind generates the utility classes
4. Update `WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` in `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` to use `text-status-in-progress`, `text-status-in-review`, `text-status-done` instead of the hardcoded palette colors
5. `backlog` and `canceled` already use `text-muted-foreground` (semantic) ÔÇö leave those as-is
6. Run `bun run typecheck` to verify

## Problem

`WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` in `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` uses `text-amber-500`, `text-purple-500`, `text-green-500` ÔÇö raw Tailwind palette utilities. The project's CLAUDE.md says "Never hardcode colors at all costs" and the adjacent `STATUS_CONFIG` in the same file uses semantic tokens (`text-destructive`, `text-status-unread`, `text-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`).

## Fix

1. Read `apps/web/src/global.css` to understand the existing CSS variable structure (look for `:root`, `.dark`, `@theme` blocks, and existing `--status-*` variables)
2. Add three new CSS variables for workflow status colors. Place them near existing status color variables. Name them `--status-in-progress`, `--status-in-review`, `--status-done`. For values:
   - `--status-in-progress` should be amber/warning-like (check if there's an existing `--warning` variable to reference, otherwise use an oklch amber)
   - `--status-in-review` should be purple (check if there's a `--primary` or purple variable, otherwise use oklch purple)
   - `--status-done` should be green/success-like (check if there's a `--success` variable, otherwise use oklch green)
   - Define in both `:root` (light) and `.dark` (dark) themes
3. Add the corresponding `--color-status-*` entries in the `@theme` block so Tailwind generates the utility classes
4. Update `WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` in `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` to use `text-status-in-progress`, `text-status-in-review`, `text-status-done` instead of the hardcoded palette colors
5. `backlog` and `canceled` already use `text-muted-foreground` (semantic) ÔÇö leave those as-is
6. Run `bun run typecheck` to verify

feat: add workspace workflow status (backlog/in-progress/in-review/done/canceled)

Adds a Linear-style `status` column to workspaces that tracks workflow phase,
orthogonal to the existing `state` (lifecycle) and `session_status` (activity).

- Schema: new `status TEXT` column with migration, index, and stats counts
- Auto-progression: sticky/flow semantics via `autoProgressStatus()` ÔÇö PR
  discovery triggers in-review, PR merge triggers done, archive forces done
- Manual override: `updateWorkspaceStatus` mutation (WS + HTTP PATCH)
- Frontend: optimistic mutation hook, semantic CSS color tokens, status config
- Validation: Zod runtime parse on WS mutation path for defense-in-depth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

gh pr create --base main --title "feat: workspace workflow status (backlog Ôćĺ in-progress Ôćĺ in-review Ôćĺ done)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Adds a `status` column to workspaces tracking workflow phase (backlog, in-progress, in-review, done, canceled) ÔÇö orthogonal to existing `state` (lifecycle) and `session_status` (agent activity)
- Auto-progression with sticky/flow semantics: PR discovery Ôćĺ in-review, PR merge Ôćĺ done, archive Ôćĺ done (forced), unarchive Ôćĺ in-progress
- New `workspace-status.service.ts` with `autoProgressStatus()` (respects sticky states, prevents regression) and `setWorkspaceStatus()` (user override)
- Frontend: optimistic `useUpdateWorkspaceStatus` mutation hook, `WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` with semantic CSS color tokens, Zod-validated WS mutation path

## Test plan

- [x] TypeScript type check passes (`bun run typecheck`)
- [x] All 390 backend unit tests pass (`bun run test:backend`)
- [ ] Manual: verify new workspaces default to `in-progress`
- [ ] Manual: verify archiving sets status to `done`, unarchiving restores to `in-progress`
- [ ] Manual: verify PR discovery auto-progresses to `in-review`
- [ ] Manual: verify sticky states (backlog, canceled) resist auto-progression
